operating_state: Total revenues decreased to $51.9 billion in Q3 2023 from $63.5 billion in Q3 2022, driven by lower upstream realizations and downstream margins amid reduced commodity pricing. Net oil-equivalent production increased to 3.15 million barrels per day, up 4% from prior year, driven by PDC acquisition adding production and Permian Basin growth, partially offset by field declines and turnaround impacts.
cost_structure: Operating expenses remained relatively flat while purchased crude oil and products costs declined reflecting lower commodity prices. Capital expenditures increased significantly with elevated upstream spending and acquisitions driving higher investment levels compared to prior year periods.
material_events: Completed PDC Energy acquisition for $6.5 billion in August 2023, adding acreage in Denver-Julesburg and Permian basins. Announced definitive agreement to acquire Hess Corporation for approximately $53 billion with expected closing in first half 2024. Repurchased $11.5 billion of shares during nine-month period and paid dividends of $8.5 billion, with quarterly dividend increased to $1.51 per share.
macro_exposures: Brent crude oil averaged $87 per barrel in Q3 2023 versus higher prior year levels, with prices supported by OPEC+ production cuts but facing demand uncertainty. U.S. Henry Hub natural gas prices averaged $2.49 per MCF in first nine months 2023 versus significantly higher prior year due to elevated inventory levels. Israel-Hamas conflict led to shutdown of Tamar gas field operations in October 2023.
forward_outlook: FGP/WPMP project in Kazakhstan expected to begin startup in first half 2024 with full production ramp by first half 2025. Share repurchases expected around $3 billion in Q4 2023 depending on Hess proxy statement timing. PDC acquisition expected to generate annual synergies and incremental free cash flow with accretion to return metrics.
uncertainty_profile: Hess acquisition subject to stockholder approval and regulatory approvals with completion targeted for first half 2024. Integration risks exist for both acquisitions including potential failure to realize anticipated synergies. Venezuelan operations continue under general license with uncertain future results, while potential sanctions could impact Caspian Pipeline Consortium operations.
